item.title: Broadcom to lose more than $300bn in market value as revenue forecast disappoints
item.description: Shares in chip company falls 14 per cent in pre-market trading on Thursday

item.title: Elon Musk shifts social media focus from SpaceX to UK far-right politics
item.description: Tech billionaire has made 110 posts, retweets and replies about British affairs in the space of a week

item.title: Lex Greensill banned from UK directorships for 9 years
item.description: Financier’s disqualification from Insolvency Service comes five years after Greensill Capital collapse

item.title: Trafigura warns oil at ‘inflection point’ as Iran war stokes bumper half-year profits
item.description: Commodity trader’s net profit more than doubles to $4.1bn for October to March period

item.title: Rémy Cointreau shares bounce on plan to revive cognac sales
item.description: Spirits group projects €100mn profit boost over next 3 years as it looks for growth in emerging markets and travel retail

item.title: Americans lead AI data centre backlash, global poll finds
item.description: US has lowest support among 15 large economies for infrastructure expansion

item.title: Partners Group prepares to cap withdrawals at US fund for wealthy individuals
item.description: Swiss group faces increasing strains as redemption requests from investors rise

item.title: Nvidia’s CPU move and China’s EV makers go solid
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: FirstFT: Trump’s US manufacturing push falters
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Russian drone warfare and SpaceX IPO

item.title: Accelerating Business
item.description: A monthly series that examines how the legal ecosystem uses new technologies to serve fast-changing business needs. This time: how legal tech stalwarts plan to maintain their service to clients as challengers mount incursions

item.title: Europe’s Best Employers
item.description: In the report, we look at defence groups rise up in the FT’s employer ranking; Amsterdam as an attractive employment hub; engineering’s gender gap; debate over work-life balance in start-ups; Americans look abroad for the longer term

item.title: Kirkland & Ellis and Palantir to build AI tool to assist private equity firms
item.description: Technology to be used to advise buyout groups on bringing in money from investors such as public pension funds

item.title: America’s ‘other’ economy tells a different growth story
item.description: Investors should pay attention to the struggles of the consumer packaged goods industry

item.title: AI cyber security risk ‘top of list’ for banking threats, says UK regulator
item.description: Sam Woods, the outgoing chief of the PRA, says he is ‘very concerned’ about vulnerabilities in lenders’ IT systems

item.title: The Knicks’ Wall Street odyssey
item.description: How NBA owner James Dolan righted the ship at his sports teams and business empire

item.title: Indian stocks lose out to Asian rivals in global hunt for AI winners
item.description: Exchanges in Taiwan and South Korea have overtaken India’s in the past week as the two countries’ chipmakers surge

item.title: Nvidia takes AI battle from the data centre to the laptop
item.description: Chipmaker opens new front in rivalry with Apple, Intel, AMD and Qualcomm

item.title: Anthropic’s relentless race to the top
item.description: Can the AI company hold on to its ethical founding principles as it strides to market with its most powerful and unnerving tool yet?

item.title: How we compiled the 2026 FT-Nikkei Investing in America rankings
item.description: Methodology behind the latest edition of the rankings that reveal the top US cities for international business

item.title: France’s €110bn AI boom tests Macron’s tech ambitions
item.description: Investors warn approvals and local opposition could slow France’s massive data centre build-out

item.title: Big Four stronger than ever as UK audit clampdown eases
item.description: The firms have emerged with their dominance entrenched and their businesses enhanced

item.title: Javier Milei: Argentina invites AI to free itself
item.description: As we enter a new era of technology, AI must be permitted to develop without premature regulation

item.title: City of London office rents close in on West End highs
item.description: Demand for space and limited supply is driving up prices in the business district

item.title: Global flight of the wealthy slows sharply as political and tax worries ease
item.description: UK had deep drop in rich people shifting jurisdiction after non-dom abolition played out

item.title: World Cup sparks betting battle between sportsbooks and prediction markets
item.description: Gambling firms offer new features in a bid to stop prediction markets muscling in on football betting bonanza

item.title: Trump’s pledge to unleash a ‘golden age’ of US manufacturing sputters
item.description: Spending on new factories has fallen while industry executives point to uneven recovery

item.title: Russia could use drone edge to threaten Baltics, warns Latvian general
item.description: Military chief says Moscow has gained technological advantage that it may use to attack Nato by end 2028

item.title: Defence companies climb FT ranking of top European employers
item.description: Appeal burnished by newfound purpose: ‘Our employees know why they get up in the morning’

item.title: Boston tops FT-Nikkei ranking as global companies seek skilled workers
item.description: International groups pick Massachusetts capital for intellectual wealth and quality of life, despite higher costs

item.title: Challengers test defences of legal tech stalwarts
item.description: Incumbents are upgrading products, while emphasising authoritative content and expertise that legal teams can trust
